sentences.sentence: In the Native American Navajo nation, which sprawls across four states in the American south-west, the native language is dying.
sentences.translation: 在横跨美国西南部四个州的纳瓦霍原住民族中，其本土语言正在消亡。

structure.text: In the Native American Navajo nation
which sprawls across four states in the American south-west
the native language is dying

difficult_points: which 修饰 Navajo nation，sprawl 表示地域横跨。
is dying 是现在进行时，表示语言正在逐渐消失。

sentences.sentence: Most of its speakers are middle-aged or elderly.
sentences.translation: 它的大多数使用者都已步入中年或老年。

structure.text: Most of its speakers
are middle-aged or elderly

difficult_points: its 指代 Navajo language。
middle-aged 与 elderly 是并列表语。

sentences.sentence: Although many students take classes in Navajo, the schools are run in English.
sentences.translation: 尽管许多学生上纳瓦霍语课，学校却使用英语运营。

structure.text: Although many students take classes in Navajo
the schools are run in English

difficult_points: Although 引导让步从句。
are run 是被动语态，in English 说明教学和管理语言。

sentences.sentence: Street signs, supermarket goods and even their own newspaper are all in English.
sentences.translation: 街道标志、超市商品，甚至他们自己的报纸，全都是英文的。

structure.text: Street signs, supermarket goods and even their own newspaper
are all in English

difficult_points: 三个名词短语并列作主语。
even 强调连自己的报纸也使用英语。

sentences.sentence: Not surprisingly, linguists doubt that any native speakers of Navajo will remain in a hundred years' time.
sentences.translation: 不出所料，语言学家怀疑一百年后还会有纳瓦霍语母语者存在。

structure.text: Not surprisingly
linguists doubt
that any native speakers of Navajo will remain in a hundred years' time

difficult_points: that 从句作 doubt 的宾语。
in a hundred years' time 表示未来时间点。

sentences.sentence: Navajo is far from alone.
sentences.translation: 纳瓦霍语远非唯一面临这种处境的语言。

structure.text: Navajo
is far from alone

difficult_points: far from 表示“远非”，不是空间距离。
alone 指语言面临消亡的处境。

sentences.sentence: Half the world's 6,800 languages are likely to vanish within two generations—that's one language lost every ten days.
sentences.translation: 全世界6800种语言中有一半可能在两代人内消失——也就是每十天消失一种语言。

structure.text: Half the world's 6,800 languages
are likely to vanish within two generations
that's one language lost every ten days

difficult_points: are likely to 表示可能性。
破折号后换算消失速度，lost 是省略被动结构。

sentences.sentence: Never before has the planet's linguistic diversity shrunk at such a pace.
sentences.translation: 这个星球的语言多样性从未以如此速度缩减。

structure.text: Never before
has the planet's linguistic diversity shrunk
at such a pace

difficult_points: Never before 置于句首引起部分倒装。
at such a pace 表示缩减速度之快。

sentences.sentence: "At the moment, we are heading for about three or four languages dominating the world," says Mark Pagel, an evolutionary biologist at the University of Reading.
sentences.translation: “目前，我们正走向由大约三四种语言主导世界的局面，”雷丁大学进化生物学家马克·佩格尔说。

structure.text: At the moment
we are heading for about three or four languages dominating the world
says Mark Pagel
an evolutionary biologist at the University of Reading

difficult_points: heading for 表示走向某种结果。
languages dominating the world 是复合宾语结构，说明未来格局。

sentences.sentence: "It's a mass extinction, and whether we will ever rebound from the loss is difficult to know."
sentences.translation: “这是一场大规模灭绝，而我们是否能从这种损失中恢复，很难知道。”

structure.text: It's a mass extinction
whether we will ever rebound from the loss
is difficult to know

difficult_points: whether 从句作主语，说明难以判断的内容。
rebound from 表示从损失中恢复。

sentences.sentence: Isolation breeds linguistic diversity: as a result, the world is peppered with languages spoken by only a few people.
sentences.translation: 隔绝孕育语言多样性；因此，世界各地散布着只有少数人使用的语言。

structure.text: Isolation breeds linguistic diversity
as a result
the world is peppered with languages spoken by only a few people
spoken by only a few people

difficult_points: 冒号后解释前句结果，spoken 过去分词修饰 languages。
peppered with 是比喻性的“散布着”。

sentences.sentence: Only 250 languages have more than a million speakers, and at least 3,000 have fewer than 2,500.
sentences.translation: 只有250种语言拥有超过一百万使用者，至少3000种的使用者少于2500人。

structure.text: Only 250 languages have more than a million speakers
and at least 3,000 have fewer than 2,500

difficult_points: more than 与 fewer than 构成数量对比。
第二分句省略 speakers，避免重复。

sentences.sentence: It is not necessarily these small languages that are about to disappear.
sentences.translation: 即将消失的不一定正是这些小语种。

structure.text: It is not necessarily these small languages that are about to disappear
these small languages
are about to disappear

difficult_points: it is...that... 是强调结构，not necessarily 表示不一定。
be about to 表示即将发生。

sentences.sentence: Navajo is considered endangered despite having 150,000 speakers.
sentences.translation: 尽管有15万使用者，纳瓦霍语仍被认为濒危。

structure.text: Navajo
is considered endangered
despite having 150,000 speakers

difficult_points: is considered 是被动语态，endangered 是补语。
despite 后接动名词 having，表示让步。

sentences.sentence: What makes a language endangered is not just the number of speakers, but how old they are.
sentences.translation: 使一种语言濒危的不仅是使用者数量，还有他们的年龄。

structure.text: What makes a language endangered
is not just the number of speakers, but how old they are

difficult_points: What 从句作主语，说明导致濒危的因素。
not just...but... 连接数量与年龄两个因素。

sentences.sentence: If it is spoken by children, it is relatively safe.
sentences.translation: 如果儿童也使用这种语言，它就相对安全。

structure.text: If it is spoken by children
it is relatively safe

difficult_points: 被动语态 is spoken 强调语言被儿童使用。
it 在两个分句中均指该语言。

sentences.sentence: The critically endangered languages are those that are only spoken by the elderly, according to Michael Krauss, director of the Alaska Native Language Center, in Fairbanks.
sentences.translation: 据费尔班克斯阿拉斯加原住民语言中心主任迈克尔·克劳斯说，极度濒危的语言是那些只有老年人使用的语言。

structure.text: The critically endangered languages
are those
that are only spoken by the elderly
according to Michael Krauss, director of the Alaska Native Language Center, in Fairbanks

difficult_points: those 指代 languages，that 定语从句限定其特征。
according to 短语引出观点来源及身份。

sentences.sentence: Why do people reject the language of their parents?
sentences.translation: 人们为什么会拒绝父母的语言？

structure.text: Why do people reject the language of their parents?

difficult_points: Why 疑问副词询问原因。
of their parents 修饰 language。

sentences.sentence: It begins with a crisis of confidence, when a small community finds itself alongside a larger, wealthier society, says Nicholas Ostler, of Britain's Foundation for Endangered Languages, in Bath.
sentences.translation: 这始于信心危机：当一个小社区发现自己与更大、更富裕的社会相邻时，英国濒危语言基金会的尼古拉斯·奥斯特勒说。

structure.text: It begins with a crisis of confidence
when a small community finds itself alongside a larger, wealthier society
says Nicholas Ostler, of Britain's Foundation for Endangered Languages, in Bath

difficult_points: when 从句说明信心危机发生的情境。
find oneself alongside 表示发现自己处于某种环境。

sentences.sentence: "People lose faith in their culture," he says.
sentences.translation: “人们会对自己的文化失去信心，”他说。

structure.text: People lose faith in their culture
he says

difficult_points: lose faith in 表示不再信任或相信。
he 指 Nicholas Ostler。

sentences.sentence: "When the next generation reaches their teens, they might not want to be inducted into the old traditions."
sentences.translation: “当下一代进入青少年时期时，他们可能不愿被纳入旧传统。”

structure.text: When the next generation reaches their teens
they might not want to be inducted into the old traditions

difficult_points: When 从句说明年龄阶段，might 表示可能性。
be inducted into 是被动表达，意为被引入传统。

sentences.sentence: The change is not always voluntary.
sentences.translation: 这种改变并不总是自愿的。

structure.text: The change
is not always voluntary

difficult_points: not always 表示并非每次都如此。
voluntary 指语言转换并非出于自由选择。

sentences.sentence: Quite often, governments try to kill off a minority language by banning its use in public or discouraging its use in schools, all to promote national unity.
sentences.translation: 政府经常通过禁止少数语言在公共场合使用或阻止其在学校使用来消灭它，目的都是促进国家统一。

structure.text: Quite often
governments try to kill off a minority language
by banning its use in public or discouraging its use in schools
all to promote national unity

difficult_points: try to kill off 表示试图彻底消灭。
by 后并列 banning 和 discouraging，to promote 说明政策目的。

sentences.sentence: The former US policy of running Indian reservation schools in English, for example, effectively put languages such as Navajo on the danger list.
sentences.translation: 例如，美国过去用英语运营印第安人保留地学校的政策，实际上把纳瓦霍语等语言列入了危险名单。

structure.text: The former US policy of running Indian reservation schools in English
effectively put languages such as Navajo on the danger list

difficult_points: of running... 是 policy 的动名词修饰。
put A on the danger list 表示使语言面临危险。

sentences.sentence: But Salikoko Mufwene, who chairs the Linguistics department at the University of Chicago, argues that the deadliest weapon is not government policy but economic globalisation.
sentences.translation: 但芝加哥大学语言学系主任萨利科科·穆夫韦内认为，最致命的武器不是政府政策，而是经济全球化。

structure.text: Salikoko Mufwene
who chairs the Linguistics department at the University of Chicago
argues that the deadliest weapon is not government policy but economic globalisation

difficult_points: who 修饰 Mufwene；that 从句作 argues 宾语。
not...but... 对比两种语言消亡原因。

sentences.sentence: "Native Americans have not lost pride in their language, but they have had to adapt to socio-economic pressures," he says.
sentences.translation: “美国原住民并未失去对自己语言的自豪，但他们不得不适应社会经济压力，”他说。

structure.text: Native Americans have not lost pride in their language
but they have had to adapt to socio-economic pressures

difficult_points: but 连接自豪感仍在与被迫适应现实的对比。
have had to 表示过去至今一直不得不。

sentences.sentence: "They cannot refuse to speak English if most commercial activity is in English." But are languages worth saving?
sentences.translation: “如果大多数商业活动都使用英语，他们就不能拒绝说英语。”但语言值得拯救吗？

structure.text: They cannot refuse to speak English
if most commercial activity is in English
But are languages worth saving?

difficult_points: if 从句说明不能拒绝使用英语的条件。
worth saving 是 worth + 动名词结构，表示值得拯救。

sentences.sentence: At the very least, there is a loss of data for the study of languages and their evolution, which relies on comparisons between languages, both living and dead.
sentences.translation: 至少，语言及其演变研究会损失数据，而这种研究依赖于对活着和已经消亡的语言进行比较。

structure.text: At the very least
there is a loss of data for the study of languages and their evolution
which relies on comparisons between languages, both living and dead

difficult_points: there is 是存在句，which 指语言研究。
both living and dead 补充说明比较对象。

sentences.sentence: When an unwritten and unrecorded language disappears, it is lost to science.
sentences.translation: 当一种没有文字、没有记录的语言消失时，它也就从科学中消失了。

structure.text: When an unwritten and unrecorded language disappears
it is lost to science

difficult_points: unwritten 和 unrecorded 并列修饰 language。
be lost to science 表示科学研究无法再获得该语言资料。

sentences.sentence: Language is also intimately bound up with culture, so it may be difficult to preserve one without the other.
sentences.translation: 语言也与文化密切相连，因此很难在不保留文化的情况下单独保存语言，反之亦然。

structure.text: Language is also intimately bound up with culture
so it may be difficult to preserve one without the other

difficult_points: be bound up with 表示密切相关。
one...the other 分别指 language 和 culture。

sentences.sentence: "If a person shifts from Navajo to English, they lose something," Mufwene says.
sentences.translation: “如果一个人从纳瓦霍语转向英语，他们会失去一些东西，”穆夫韦内说。

structure.text: If a person shifts from Navajo to English
they lose something
Mufwene says

difficult_points: If 从句说明语言转换的条件。
they 指代 a person，是单数 they 的用法。

sentences.sentence: "Moreover, the loss of diversity may also deprive us of different ways of looking at the world," says Pagel.
sentences.translation: “此外，多样性的丧失还可能使我们失去看待世界的不同方式，”佩格尔说。

structure.text: the loss of diversity
may also deprive us of different ways of looking at the world
says Pagel

difficult_points: deprive somebody of 表示使某人失去某物。
of looking at the world 是 ways 的动名词修饰。

expressions.text: ways of looking at the world

sentences.sentence: There is mounting evidence that learning a language produces physiological changes in the brain.
sentences.translation: 越来越多的证据表明，学习一门语言会引起大脑的生理变化。

structure.text: There is mounting evidence
that learning a language produces physiological changes in the brain

difficult_points: There is 存在句，mounting 表示证据不断增加。
learning a language 是 that 从句的动名词主语。

sentences.sentence: "Your brain and mine are different from the brain of someone who speaks French, for instance," Pagel says, and this could affect our thoughts and perceptions.
sentences.translation: “例如，你的大脑和我的大脑与说法语的人的大脑不同，”佩格尔说，而这可能影响我们的思想和感知。

structure.text: Your brain and mine are different from the brain of someone who speaks French
who speaks French
and this could affect our thoughts and perceptions

difficult_points: mine 是名词性物主代词，代替 my brain。
who 修饰 someone；this 指大脑差异这一事实。

sentences.sentence: "The patterns and connections we make among various concepts may be structured by the linguistic habits of our community."
sentences.translation: “我们在不同概念之间形成的模式和联系，可能受到所在社区语言习惯的塑造。”

structure.text: The patterns and connections we make among various concepts
we make among various concepts
may be structured by the linguistic habits of our community

difficult_points: we make 是省略关系代词的定语从句，修饰 patterns and connections。
may be structured 是情态动词加被动语态。

sentences.sentence: So despite linguists' best efforts, many languages will disappear over the next century.
sentences.translation: 所以，尽管语言学家竭尽全力，未来一个世纪仍会有许多语言消失。

structure.text: despite linguists' best efforts
many languages will disappear over the next century

difficult_points: despite 后接名词短语表示让步。
will disappear 表示预测的未来结果。

sentences.sentence: But a growing interest in cultural identity may prevent the direst predictions from coming true.
sentences.translation: 但人们日益增长的文化认同兴趣，可能阻止最悲观的预测成为现实。

structure.text: a growing interest in cultural identity
may prevent the direst predictions from coming true

difficult_points: prevent + 宾语 + from doing 表示阻止某事发生。
direst 是 dire 的最高级，表示最严峻。

sentences.sentence: "The key to fostering diversity is for people to learn their ancestral tongue, as well as the dominant language," says Doug Whalen, founder and president of the Endangered Language Fund in New Haven, Connecticut.
sentences.translation: “促进多样性的关键，是让人们学习祖先的语言以及主导语言，”康涅狄格州纽黑文濒危语言基金创始人兼主席道格·惠伦说。

structure.text: The key to fostering diversity
is for people to learn their ancestral tongue, as well as the dominant language
says Doug Whalen, founder and president of the Endangered Language Fund in New Haven, Connecticut

difficult_points: the key to 后接动名词 fostering。
for people to learn 是不定式复合结构，as well as 表示并列补充。

sentences.sentence: "Most of these languages will not survive without a large degree of bilingualism," he says.
sentences.translation: “没有相当程度的双语使用，这些语言大多无法生存，”他说。

structure.text: Most of these languages will not survive without a large degree of bilingualism
he says

difficult_points: without 短语说明语言存续所需的条件。
a large degree of 表示相当程度。

sentences.sentence: In New Zealand, classes for children have slowed the erosion of Maori and rekindled interest in the language.
sentences.translation: 在新西兰，为儿童开设的课程减缓了毛利语的流失，并重新点燃了人们对这种语言的兴趣。

structure.text: In New Zealand
classes for children
have slowed the erosion of Maori and rekindled interest in the language

difficult_points: have slowed 与 rekindled 并列。
the erosion of Maori 是毛利语逐渐流失的名词表达。

sentences.sentence: A similar approach in Hawaii has produced about 8,000 new speakers of Polynesian languages in the past few years.
sentences.translation: 夏威夷的类似做法在过去几年培养了约8000名波利尼西亚语言的新使用者。

structure.text: A similar approach in Hawaii
has produced
about 8,000 new speakers of Polynesian languages
in the past few years

difficult_points: has produced 是现在完成时，强调近期成果。
new speakers of Polynesian languages 表示新增使用者。

sentences.sentence: In California, "apprentice" programmes have provided life support to several indigenous languages.
sentences.translation: 在加利福尼亚，“学徒”项目为几种本土语言提供了生命支持。

structure.text: In California
"apprentice" programmes
have provided life support to several indigenous languages

difficult_points: life support 是比喻，指帮助语言存续。
have provided 表示项目已产生持续效果。

sentences.sentence: Volunteer "apprentices" pair up with one of the last living speakers of a Native American tongue to learn a traditional skill such as basket weaving, with instruction exclusively in the endangered language.
sentences.translation: 志愿“学徒”与最后几位仍在世的美洲原住民语言使用者之一结对，学习篮子编织等传统技能，整个教学过程只使用濒危语言。

structure.text: Volunteer "apprentices" pair up with one of the last living speakers of a Native American tongue
to learn a traditional skill such as basket weaving
with instruction exclusively in the endangered language

difficult_points: pair up with 表示结对合作。
to learn 说明结对目的，with 短语说明教学语言。

sentences.sentence: After about 300 hours of training they are generally sufficiently fluent to transmit the language to the next generation.
sentences.translation: 经过约300小时训练后，他们通常能熟练到足以把这种语言传给下一代。

structure.text: After about 300 hours of training
they are generally sufficiently fluent
to transmit the language to the next generation

difficult_points: sufficiently...to... 表示熟练程度足以完成传承。
to transmit 是结果不定式。

sentences.sentence: But Mufwene says that preventing a language dying out is not the same as giving it new life by using it every day.
sentences.translation: 但穆夫韦内说，阻止一种语言消亡，并不等同于通过每天使用它来赋予它新生命。

structure.text: Mufwene says
that preventing a language dying out is not the same as giving it new life by using it every day
preventing a language dying out
by using it every day

difficult_points: 两个动名词短语 preventing 与 giving 构成 not the same as 对比。
by using 说明赋予新生命的方式。

sentences.sentence: "Preserving a language is more like preserving fruits in a jar," he says.
sentences.translation: “保存一种语言更像是把水果保存在罐子里，”他说。

structure.text: Preserving a language
is more like preserving fruits in a jar

difficult_points: 两个 preserving 动名词形成比喻对照。
more like 表示更类似于。

sentences.sentence: However, preservation can bring a language back from the dead.
sentences.translation: 然而，保存工作能够使一种语言死而复生。

structure.text: However
preservation
can bring a language back from the dead

difficult_points: bring back from the dead 是使语言复活的比喻表达。
can 表示可能性或能力。

sentences.sentence: There are examples of languages that have survived in written form and then been revived by later generations.
sentences.translation: 有些语言以书面形式存活下来，后来又被后代复兴。

structure.text: There are examples of languages
that have survived in written form and then been revived by later generations

difficult_points: that 从句中 have survived 与 been revived 并列。
been revived 是现在完成时被动语态。

sentences.sentence: But a written form is essential for this, so the mere possibility of revival has led many speakers of endangered languages to develop systems of writing where none existed before.
sentences.translation: 但书面形式对此至关重要，因此仅仅是复兴的可能性，就促使许多濒危语言使用者在原本没有文字的地方建立书写体系。

structure.text: a written form is essential for this
so the mere possibility of revival has led many speakers of endangered languages to develop systems of writing
where none existed before

difficult_points: so 连接因果结果；lead + 宾语 + to do 表示促使某人做某事。
where none existed before 表示过去没有书写系统的地方。
